column2: Portfolio health, demographics & geography — 10,000 customers across France, Spain & Germany
Where churn happens, and to whom

column9: ??  Germany churns at 32.4% — nearly 2× France/Spain. Holds 25% of customers but generates 40% of all churn.
??  Female churn 25.1% vs Male 16.5%. A 1.5× gap that holds across all 3 geographies.
??  Age 50-60 is the peak churn cohort at 56.0%. Risk climbs steeply from age 40 onward.
??  Active members churn at 14% vs 27% for inactive — the most lever-able retention input.
??  Tenure has no effect: long-tenured customers churn at the same rate as new ones (~20%).
??  See Deep-Dive sheet for product / balance / segment analysis.
